So who here is excited about supplements?
Man.
Who here excited about my logo I got done?
I was pretty excited.
I don't know about you guys, but this is how my process works.
Step number one is I get a cool idea and I got to find the right domain name, right?
That's like number one for me.
I can't start working on anything to have the right domain.
I don't know if you guys are that way.
But I'll sit there for hours and days and weeks and sometimes years until I find the right domain.
As soon as I get the domain, I'm like, okay, I got it.
Now I need a logo.
That's my next step is I have to get a logo.
I can't visualize anything until I have a logo.
I don't know if that's just me.
So I got this logo done and I was like, okay, now I can create this product.
So we are going to be probably in the next, before then this year, so probably next month or so, we're going to be doing like a six week more in-depth training on how to do supplements.
All of you guys are invited to be part of that for free.
I'm not going to charge you guys.
Anyone who's watching this video, you guys have to pay for it because you're not here right now.
But you guys have a chance to go through the train for free because you sacrificed, you came out here to Boise.
But we're going to go through a lot more detail of everything I'm going to be talking about over the next 90 minutes or so.
Really my goal for the next 90 minutes is, I just want to tell you guys kind of the journey I went through in this whole supplement business.
And I want to do it for two reasons.
One, because I think for a lot of you guys, it's going to get you excited and make you want to go do supplements.
And some of you guys, it's going to make you realize that you don't want to do supplements.
And so I want to help divide that line right now.
So you don't go running this direction.
If you find out later, you don't want to do it.
But some of you guys are going to see that this is really fun, really exciting thing, and you're going to want to become part of it.
So that's kind of the game plan.
All right, so my supplement journey began back when I was in high school.
And I was a wrestler.
A lot of you guys know that.
And I was a state champion my junior year.
And I was training for national stuff on my senior year.
And I wanted to learn a whole bunch.
You know, I wanted to become the best I could.
And so I was reading everything I could find.
And back then, there was a magazine that I still think is the best bodybuilding magazine ever.
It was called Muscle Media.
Who here remembers muscle media?
That's it.
How sad is that?
OK, who here remembers Bill Phillips, Body for Life?
So Body for Life was what happened after muscle media.
So muscle media was a magazine that Bill Phillips ran.
And it was one of the first ones back in the day.
And it was like the first one, it wasn't all like the big, huge, ripped dudes.
It was kind of like this thing where it was in the middle ground.
And I really gravitated towards it.
And I would read it all the time.
I just loved it.
And I don't know, a couple of years into me reading it, I remember Bill Phillips put out this offer.
And Bill was ahead of his time.
And he did some amazing marketing stuff that got me a high school kid with $0 to pull a lot of money out of my parents' pockets and buy things, which is always the sign of a really good marketer.
So anyway, he had this supplement review guide.
Does anyone here have that supplement review guide?
It was $5.
It was like 500 pages.
You just pay $5 shipping handling.
He mailed you out saying it was 500 pages big.
Nobody here has got it.
I still have them on my shelf.
Every year he did them.
He had the supplement review guide year one, then year two.
I think he did it four or five years before I ended up selling Body for Life, EAS, and muscle media, and everything kind of went away.
But it was amazing.
I remember getting this book.
And it was so fat.
And I was reading through it.
And what was cool is that, you look how Bill Phillips did it.
He went the whole, like we talked about yesterday, started the expert route first, right?
Build up this name, build up this distribution channel.
And they started adding products to there.
And he launched this whole company called EAS.
Do you guys remember EAS?
Do you remember he used to walk into GNC?
And everything in GNC was EAS.
Little like every, like all EAS, and there's a little corner with some weird crap on the side.
Because that's how big of a following, and how big of a brand he built.
It was amazing.
But you read the supplement guide.
It was like 500 pages.
And he would talk about every single supplement in the category.
He'd go creatines, and then the proteins, and then just every single thing.
And he would just talk about stuff.
And it's funny because as a 12-year-old kid, man, I thought he was the most genius person in the world.
And as a marketer, I think he is.
But he was just talking like as if I was writing it.
Like he's just totally his opinion.
I don't think there was any science backing anything he said.
He was just talking about why this protein sucked, this one sucked, all of them are horrible, except for EAS, man.
And the EAS stuff is the best.
And I never knew that he owned EAS.
I didn't ever make that connection.
All I knew is that I needed a lot of protein, 2 grams per body pound per day, because that's what Bill Phillips said.
And I had to have EAS because it was the only kind.
And so I'd go in there, I'd buy all this tons and tons of protein.
And then he'd talk about amino acids.
And he'd talk about creatines.
He'd talk about, and I was literally buying every single thing in GNC that he'd recommended in this huge book.
I had the book I still have, like I said, that has highlighted, circled every single thing.
And I was just buying anything he said because I believed in it.
And I was taking it.
And I just kind of became obsessed with taking supplements.
And again, that was before I had any money to my name.
I was using my parents' money to buy it.
But it was just cool to see this person who, looking back now, had taken his expertise, built a huge following, and then used that as a tool to sell a whole bunch of his supplements.
And really, really exciting.
And I look at his kind of funny Fast Forward Now, I don't know, 15, 20 years later.
Some of my friends, like Brent and John, make fun of me.
Some of my friends are grueling with me.
But like, every morning still to this day, I wake up.
And I probably take, I don't know, I send you guys pictures of it before.
I probably take like 50 pills every morning, just because I've been sold on every single one of them.
I'm like the easiest person in the world to sell.
I'm going to show you guys, this is a, you guys remember Simon who's here at our last event.
This is his new supplement that he just finished.
He totally sold me on it.
Like, I'm going to start taking it every day too.
Because I just like, I believe in things and someone sells me, I'm like, I'm in.
And then I can't stop taking it.
And I remember like, half the time I take this stuff and I have no idea what this does.
But I remember the guy who sold it to me and it like, he convinced me that I needed it forever.
And so I still take all of them every day.
But I just like, I love the supplement market because of that, like something I believe in, it's passionate.
I've had a lot of results from supplements, and so I love it.
And so that's kind of where it started from initially.
But I never knew there was much money in it until about a year, maybe two years into my internet marketing business.
And I was learning all this stuff.
I had information products.
I was kind of learning and just doing all these cool things.
And then about the time, there was this book that came out.
And everyone started like, whispering about this book.
And they're like, man, this book is awesome.
It's like, as fat as a phone book.
And it's $300 per book.
And I was like, whoa, that's crazy.
And then I heard one guy talk about it, and another guy talk about it.
And then Gary Halbert started talking about it.
All these people who I respect, it started talking about this book.
And how they said it was the greatest marketing book they'd ever seen, ever.
And after like five or six people talking about it, I'm like, I got to get a copy of the book.
And so I went online, and I found the guy's selling it.
And this was the book.
Look at how it's seriously like the size of a phone book, right?
And it's the story about this guy.
He was a 23-year-old kid at the time.
So who are 22, 23-year-olds?
Couple of you guys.
His name is Vincent James.
And he made $100 million.
Is that nine, 10 figures?
How many figures is that?
A lot of figures. $100 million in 23 months selling supplements through direct mail.
And I was like, that's really, really cool.
And so I was about to go on a vacation with my wife.
She was four months pregnant with our twins at the time.
So this is almost nine years ago now.
And I saw this thing.
And I went and I ordered online.

I want to read this on the cruise.
And so I paid extra money.

I opened it up.

The very first chapter, he starts going into how much money he had.

Kind of money.
I can't even fathom that this guy was making.

And he wrote a sales letter, put it in the mail.

And later, his business completely collapsed.
Some of the issues that Steve kind of talked about with his business grew too fast.
He ended up going to jail.
And in this, he talks about everything.
I was just like, I can't believe he's telling everything.

And I was fortunate enough to buy the rights to it before Vince got in big trouble again and disappeared.
And so we're the only people on Earth that can still sell this.

So that's kind of how I just got so excited about this.

Now, one thing that was interesting when I was talking to Vince.
I remember because I just assumed that it's really hard to make a supplement.
Who kind of assumes there's a lot that goes into it?
I always just kind of assumed that.
And he was just like, he told me what happened.
He said that, again, he was a young kid in his 20s.
And he had a girlfriend at the time.
And he said that one day he was hanging out.
And he was a direct marketing guy.
So he was reading Dan Kennedy books and Gary Halbert and just doing this stuff.
And he said that his girlfriend came in with a bunch of her friends.
And they kept talking about this new pill you could take that would enhance a woman's features to make them more womanly.
And these girls are just talking about it.
And he's just like, wow, these girls are really excited by this thing.
That's really interesting.
And they had a bottle of it.
And so he grabbed the bottle.
And he flipped it over.
And on the back, he looked.
And he saw something really interesting.
There was a list of every single thing that was in the bottle.
He was like, I know what's in here.
This is the entire list.
And then he went and he found somebody to make supplements.
He's like, hey, here's the supplement.
Here's everything you need.
Can you guys just do that too?
And they're like, oh, yeah, sure.
So they went and they bought the four or five ingredients.
They mixed them up in a big pot, threw in some pills.
And boom, a week later he had his very own breast enhancing product that he could put out there in the market.
He was all excited.
So he put it out there.
He wrote a little sales letter and started selling it.
And it did well for him.
He never got rich off of that product.
But at the time, everyone was talking about it.
And it was this thing.
And he kind of jumped in and made some money off it.
Now, in the interview, he never talked about the specific product.
I don't think he even talks about it in the book.
He's kind of vague.
And I'm not going to share with you exactly what it is, because it's even more embarrassing than that product.
But later he found somebody selling a supplement that would do similar things for men.
And he was like, that's interesting.
I wonder if anyone would buy that.
The same thing looked at the back of the label, saw the ingredients, called up a lab.
They mixed the ingredients, threw them in a pill, and boom, he was in business.
He was in his parents' basement with a big pen and a pad of paper, wrote out a four-page sales letter, rented a list.
This is offline stuff, pre-internet.
Rented a list, sent it out to that list.
And the very first time he sent the letter out, he tripled over on the front end.
Do you guys know what that means?
So if he spent $1,000, it means he made $3,000 back.
Now, just so you know, remember Steve was talking yesterday.
He said, if you do your first media buy and you break even, like you're going to be rich.
So for Vince, he did the very first try.
He brought in three times as much money the very first time.
He said, as soon as I saw that, he said I knew I had the next thing.

And 23 months later, he made $100 million.
Is that crazy?
Is that exciting?
And so I was just listening.
I'm like, man, this is so cool.
What kind of supplement do I want to create?
I remember going to GNC and just looking at every, and there's like a billion things in there.
And I'm looking at all of them and trying to figure it out.
And for years, I just didn't know what I wanted to create.
I kept looking at all these things.
And I just didn't know, because I was more looking at it, I was trying to figure out what I would want to sell.
And it took me a while to realize that I needed to do a little research before I just created some weird thing.
Now, a couple of interesting things that Vince also told me that I thought were exciting.
And I wrote down the math here, so I don't screw it up.
So when he was at his peak, he was selling 40,000 bottles a month per month.
And he was selling people into a continuity program.
And in fact, there's one chapter in the book that you'll find it says, I think it's called the $43 million paragraph or something like that.
And he added this paragraph to his sales letter that turned it from just people buying this to turning it into a continuity program.
He said the average person stayed out of his continuity program for four months.
So four months, he would lose 40,000 people a month, which is kind of depressing.
Every month, he would lose 40,000 people.
But this four months in the middle meant that at any given time, he had 160,000 people on continuity, paying him $40 a month.
Who knows the math on that?
I wrote it down.
I don't know what it is.
This should be really easy.
That's $16 million a month, which you do that over a year.
It's $192 million a year.
It's pretty exciting, right?
And he didn't do anything with those customers.
I asked him, did you mail him other offers and sell him stuff?
He's like, no, man, we're making so much money.
We didn't even worry about that.
I'm like, man, if you took 160,000 hotline customers, you could make this number even bigger.
But he didn't really care.
He was just killing it.
They were making so much money.
And anyway, that was kind of the story.
And as he kind of went through it, and then afterwards, he made some decisions.
He structured his business not correctly.
A lot of stuff Steve was talking about, we was talking about serious guys.
You may not think about it now as you're kind of building your business.
But there's a lot of things, how you structure your company, your customer support, all those types of things that especially you start growing, you really need to look at.
This guy was making $100 million plus a year and got taken down because just things weren't quite structured.
And again, he talks about it in the book.
He talks about it in the interview.
He's very open with all, which is kind of cool.
Because he gives us kind of a guide to learn from and know what we should be doing, how we should structure our businesses differently.
So that was kind of my first experience into this.
I'm learning all about him.
And I was excited.
But I was too much into my information business.
My expert business.
I was focusing there.
I was using all the principles he taught in here and using in our business.
And we used that to grow our company very big, pretty quickly as well.
And so I didn't even think about supplements for a long, long time.
We were focusing so much on building up our coaching and our call center and all those types of things.
That's where our focus was.
And then a couple years ago, I would say, how many years has it been?
Has anyone, three years ago we started this?
Two years ago?
About two or three years ago, we kind of sat back and we're thinking about just stuff we wanted to do.
I think most entrepreneurs go through this where you get excited and then you get bored and then you get excited.
And you know what I'm talking about?
It's horrible.
I wish we didn't do that because it always starts these crazy things.
But we're in a spot where we're just kind of bored and we just didn't know what we wanted to do with our free time.
And we're like, we should start a supplement.
And we need some guys who had a supplement they were doing really well.
And so we kind of sat back.
And we could do well with this as well.
We know marketing better.
And these guys, they're making really good money.
Let's just kind of play in this game and just figure out if we can compete.
And so I'm going to kind of walk you through the process because this is a journey that went through a couple years.
There are a lot of dumb things we did, a lot of good things we did, and hopefully it'll shortcut you guys.
So if you do want to go down this path, you'll just skip like the two years of me doing stupid things and just start right where we left off.
It's kind of like Simon, who again, this is Simon's supplement right here.
When he first joined the coaching program and my very first call with him, he came on.
He said, hey man, check out.
This is what I'm doing.
And he was in a network marketing program.
And he was trying to sell a supplement, a network marketing supplement.
He had done everything right.
Like if you look at what Steve was talking about yesterday, he went, he found offers that were similar.
He modeled them.
He made everything very, he did everything right.
The problem is he was selling somebody else a supplement.
I said, the problem is the way you're selling that, your margin is 15%.
And you need 100% to make the business work, as you understand the metrics.
And I literally sat down.
I gave him the lab, everything I'm going to show you guys right now, I gave him two years with the Mar, trial and error and everything.
And he was just like, man, that's awesome.
And he just jumped right to where we started out, picked up, and within six weeks or so he launched a supplement.
He launched it last month.
And he made a whole bunch of sales.
He's continuing making sales.
He's doing the whole scale up process now.
And it's super fun to watch them do it.
So that's kind of the goal with this presentation, is to help shortcut your guys' success and get you prepared for when we do the live coaching program where we'll dig into each piece at kind of a microscopic level so you can kind of get deeper.
All right.
So that was the first step.
So we knew some people that had a supplement and we wanted to do something kind of similar.
That was the first step.
Just like Steve talked about yesterday.
And I can't stress what he talked about you guys is 100% right.
Don't just come off like, I'm going to create a supplement that does blah, and just start selling it.
You've got to find what people are already buying.
That's one of the biggest things I can suggest for any business, info business, software business, whatever it is, sell what people are buying.
Don't just try to create stuff out of thin air and just think what people are going to buy.
Find what people are already buying.
So we found a supplement.
We know what people were buying.
And we thought they were marketing it really poorly.
And they were having success.
We thought, hey, if we come in and do it right, we can have a lot more success.
So we bought one of their products.
We looked at it.
I looked at the back, looked at their label, saw what was in it.
And then what I did is I kind of did an intermediate step.
Because I'm big on copying people, but I like to look what they're doing and modeling it.
So we saw what they were doing.
I said, I want to see how we can make this better.
So the first thing we did is we hired a chemist and said, here's these people's supplement.
This is what it does.
And we'd love for you to look at and see if there's some way you can improve upon this.
And so we had a guy, and he came back, and he gave us some feedback and said, OK, this is how I would create the formula.
This is what would make it even better.
And it was really, really cool.
And we took it to a manufacturer, and they looked at it.
And basically, they told us that it was going to cost us like $14 a bottle to create it.
I was like, that's a lot of money.
I knew from Vince James.
Vince said when he was doing his pills, he was spending between $2 and $4 per bottle.
And obviously, they were buying in a ton of margin.
I'm like, if it's $10 a bottle, that's way too much.
I need this thing to be like $5 or $6 a bottle if this is even going to work.
And I was actually at an event just like this with our Dotcom Secrets local customers.
And I don't know how it came up.
I think I just was on one of my ADD trips where I'm just talking about something.
Somehow it came up, and I was complaining about the supplement.
And this guy is sitting in the audience.
His name is Ricky Brandon, who's Ricky's right here right now.
And he was like, you know, I can actually help you out with that.
And I'm like, oh yeah.
He's like, yeah, I do a bunch of stuff with herbs and everything.
And he's like, give me your formula.
I'll see if I can do it better.
Like, all right.
We hadn't launched it yet.
I wasn't going to be able to pay $12 or $14 a bottle they wanted, whatever it was.
So Ricky took it, went back, did his magic, and came back, I don't know, a week or two later with a formula.
And I think our initial, I think it was like $6 to $7 is our initial bottle.
And it was all herbal based, which was cool because for us, it gave us this huge selling point against our evil customers who use chemicals to try to heal you.
Because we can use the herbal, the natural, all natural way.
And so he gave us that back, and I thought it was really cool.
Now, is Richard in here?
He gone.
Oh, there you are.
Yeah.
Do you have the packet they just gave you?
Do you have the packet they just gave you?
I want to show this because this is really, really cool.
So Richard's working on something right now as well.
And he went to Ricky and kind of showed him everything.
And he did this really cool ride up.
Kind of showed just for, I won't go into any details about what you're selling.
But he went through and basically said, this is what I want to do.
This is what I want to accomplish.
Ricky went and took that, him and his team went back, and they built this thing and basically said, this is what we would do.
These are things we would put into it.
This is all the science, each of the ingredients we'd use, why would we use it.
And this goes through the entire thing.
Isn't that awesome?
It goes through everything.
Shows you the dates, the schedules, the milestones, how long it would take to build his dream supplement.
Isn't that awesome?
So that's what he did for him.
That's what Ricky did for me.
And if you guys want, Ricky's here this weekend, or this week, tonight.
He's here today.
He has a form to land out as well that you guys, if you're interested in him helping you with something, you kind of fill it out, connect with him, and he can help do the same process.
But I can't tell you how much of a headache Ricky has taken off my back.
I know that because after he did an ursil for us, we did MMA night.
And I went directly, I'm like, I'm going to save a bunch of money and time, and go directly to the manufacturer.
And it has literally been the most excruciating nightmare of a process, dealing with manufacturers.
First off, because us markers, we don't want to blow a whole bunch of money on stupid stuff.
So we're like, hey, we're going to test this if it works, we'll buy a whole bunch.
So I'm like, here, I want to test this.
And they're like, well, you only gave me $5,000.
You're not a big client.
We'll get it done in six years.
That's literally their attitude.
And they give it to you wrong, they script the form.
And they don't even care because in their mind, you're so little.
They're like, well, our other clients buy $100,000 bottles a month.
I'm like, well, that's my goal, but I want to make sure someone's going to buy it.
Anyway, just a huge nightmare going this way, where Ricky took all the brunt of that for us, shopped out a thousand places, got the cheapest prices.
When three or four times, we were scaling way too fast.
And I had to call Ricky and be like, hey, man, we need 10,000 bottles like yesterday.
And he's the one freaking out, calling everybody.
Anyway, I recommend if you guys want to do a supplement, don't go directly to a manufacturer.
Go through Ricky.
His cost per bottle for us is so low right now that that's what we would get from manufacturer anyway.
So it wasn't really costing me any money at all.
I don't know how he makes money in the deal, but he seems to be happy about it.
So we're good with it.
And I think that I don't know how I can recommend him more.
So I seriously, even this one, I called you a week ago.
I was like, hey, man, I got this thing at MMA Ignite.
I just want to ship all of it to you.
And if you can just make my headaches go away.
And he's like, yeah, sure, man, no worries.
So it's just worth it.
Otherwise, what you got to do is you got to go to the manufacturer, you got to find the person on their side, you got to mix the formula.
And then it's just it's not worth it.
Don't do that part.
You guys all sold not doing that part?
OK, cool.

Second thing is, like I said, for me, because I needed a cool name for this thing.
And unfortunately, I always want to name stuff like supplementsecrets.comsecrets.
If you notice, I put secrets on everything.
That's as creative as I get with my naming, right?
So I think like nerve pain, secret, pill.
I'm really struggling on trying to figure out a cool name for this, right?
And I kept trying thing after thing.
And all my names I came up with were just stupid.
And I was just like, this is really, really hard.
I'm really good at naming information stuff.
But how do you name a pill?
And I just got really frustrated and stuck.
And one of my friends, he's got like 40 or 50 different supplements that he sells.
And his supplements, all of the coolest names.
Every time I see him, I'm like, oh man, I just want to take that pill because the name sounds so cool.
And I asked him, I'm like, how do you come up with all these awesome names?
And he's like, oh, there's this company that names them for me.
I'm like, are you serious?
He said, yeah, it's this company, their website's nameexpress.com.
And all these guys do for a living is they name stuff.
Like literally, Microsoft hires them, Apple hires them.
All these huge companies hire them.
And you pay like $200 or $300, I don't know what it is.
And you give them the name of your thing.
And they go and they sit in the brainstorm in a room with a whole bunch of guys.
And they throw up a whole bunch of names and ideas.
And they come back.
And then they go check them all and make sure they're not copyrighted and not trademarked.
And they come back and they're like, here's 10 names that are cool, that the domain names are available, and there's no copyright or trademark in place.
And I was like, awesome.
So they gave me these 10 names back.
And NuraCell was one of the names.
I was like, that one sounds awesome.
So NuraCell is what we got.
And then they had a bunch of other ones, which is kind of funny, because later on our upsell, which is NuraMend, was another one of those 10 names they gave us that we didn't use.
And I was like, oh, we bought that one.
So I got two names out of one of theirs.
So anyway, that's who I recommend.
If you guys are stuck on names at all and you do like a really cool ninja name that doesn't mean anything but seems cool, that's what they're really good at doing.
So that's how we got our name for it.

So instead of me being all fancy and trying to make up my own thing, I just modeled their page.
So this is their version of the page right here.
This is their price points.
I modeled their price points.
I said, I want to get as close to what's already working as possible.
And again, you guys, even if you're out in the self-made business, this is the exact same mindset.
Even if you're in information, if you're in software, whatever business you're in, this is how you start.
So look at their price points, look at what they were doing.
They were doing free plus shipping, or they had these other price points.
So I said, I'm going to model it.

I was like, OK, that's kind of cool.

I don't know if it's going to work in my market.

And so we went back to the drawing boards.
We made our first version.
And this is kind of looks like.
So there's a video here.

So then I took this version when it was done and we split tested it against this version, which we knew was kind of working for us.
And we started doing our test.
And lo and behold, this one crushed the other one.
It's like, sweet.
We got this thing now.
We figured out a way to beat it.

So soon as the first rebuild hit, we were at break even after optimizing and stuff like that.

But it was a lot shorter.

And then I started thinking about in an info marketing, I'm like, why am I?
Sometimes we're so smart as entrepreneurs, and we're so stupid about other things.
And I'm like, in all of our other businesses, we have upsells.
That's how we make money.
Why don't we have an upsell here, because then we would just make free money.
And I don't know why I hadn't crossed my mind for that long.
So we decided, let's make an upsell.
And we're like, well, what do we do for an upsell?
And so we started buying everyone's offers, see what they upsold, and everyone was selling different things and everything.
And I finally came down and I was like, we're selling a nerve pill to help people with their nerves.
And I'm like, I don't want to sell more pills.
I need something different.
And we started searching.
I was searching online.
One night I found this site.

And I was like, that's perfect.
And so I was like, well, I want to make a nerve cream.
But the problem is, I wasn't working through Rikki at the time.
I was working directly with the manufacturer for this one.
And it was this huge nightmare.
And they wanted X amount of minimum orders.

And it was just such a big nightmare for us to get our cream.
And I was like, you know what?
I don't even know if it's going to sell yet or not.

But this is kind of cool.
You know when we talked about value ladders?
The back of your value ladders, the best thing you could possibly give them.
And typically in info businesses, it's like, I'll just do the whole thing for you for a million dollars.
That's the best thing you can offer them.
In a weight loss business, or a nerve pain, or any business, the greatest offer you possibly have is a magic pill.
It's literally the back of the value ladder.
That's my thing about supplements.
It's the ultimate offer.
You don't have to do sit-ups.
You don't have to lose weight.
You don't have to do anything.
You just take this pill and you're going to lose weight.
You don't have to do any of these things you need to be doing.
Just take this pill and it's going to go away.
It's like pills and supplements, the greatest designed offer in the world.

It's like, the way we structured our pricing, and we haven't been able to beat this control yet, that's the thing.
Basically, you'd be stupid not to buy four bottles, right? $70 for one or four bottles for $150.
We try to push it, like, doing a decoy pricing so it pushes everyone.
And I'd say probably, I don't know the numbers, but I'd say 75%, 80% of our people are all buying the four bottle, which is really, really cool.
Because we want the highest cart value possible so we can spend all that money on the media to get people in.
And then from there, we push people to the six bottles.
And our conversion rate here is, I don't know what it is, but I remember the first round, I was just shocked how high a percent of people will buy six more bottles.
They just bought four of them by six more.
So they're getting, like, a year's supply point of sale right there, and percentage by the nerve cream, and those kind of things.
So that kind of became our funnel, and it was doing really, really well.

This one just crushed our conversion versus the video sales letter.
And so this is our control now, and it's kind of a long form thing.

As you notice here, we don't have any continuity.
So when we did this test, no, we don't have any continuity now in our episodes at all.
So we did this, and this is kind of our thing.
I have a love-hate relationship with continuity.
Mike and I have had this conversation a ton.
Continuity's really awesome on one side, but it's really really scary on the other side.
So I shy away from it, mostly because one time I almost lost everything because of it.
So I'm almost like on the other side, right, just try to stay away from as much as possible.
But we do have continuity, we had a continuity back in, if there's an exit pop that offers a free trial, it puts them into continuity.
So we do have a little bit of it, but the majority of our stuff is just straight sell.
Now, one thing that's interesting, and I don't, crap, I found the post earlier today, but Ed O'Keefe, the guy that Steve was talking about, that kind of did this, he's made a post a little while ago that was fascinating.
So this is kind of the next thing that we're gonna be testing on ours, is right now, if they buy one, three, or four, we offer them six bottles no matter what.
He'd made this tweak and he increased his cart value on average by $30, which is, if we do that right now, we'd more than double our profit, which is crazy.
But what he did is, he changed the upsell flow base stage once.
So if you bought one bottle, you were offered, I can't remember exactly, anyway, if you bought two bottles, you were offered buy two bottles, get two free, so it was four bottles, right?
If you bought four bottles, it was buy four, get four free, but it matched whatever they bought on the front end, the upsell matched it, and by doing that, he increased his overall cart value by $30 per order, okay?
And so this is kind of the process we have gone through, we keep going through, is you have this funnel, you guys, this is what gets me so excited about direct response marketing, is you're driving traffic in this thing, you're looking at it, and there's only five or six steps, right?
And you're looking at what percentage come here, what percentage come here?
Okay, I don't need to make that number bigger, what do I do?
Well, I change the video, I tweak this, I move this, I change the headline, and you increase that, right?
And you keep working all these things, and your overall goal is to get the, we call it cart value.
So cart value is, everyone that comes through here, what's the average, how much money we make on average for every single person that comes through this thing, right?
So I think right now, I don't know, let's say our average cart value is like $150, I'm not sure exactly off the top of my head what it is, and it's different for every traffic source, but let's say it's 150, right?
If I know that for every person that comes through here, I'm gonna average $150 in sales, now all the stuff that Steve was talking about, this mass media buying, going out there big and being on the scale of company fast, you can do that, because I know that every person that's gonna buy, I'm gonna make $150, that means therefore I can spend how much?
I can spend a lot of money to acquire a customer, okay?
We have 150, look at our average cost, John, what's our average cost per sell per cart?
Wasn't like 30?
No, our physical costs, like printing, shipping, like 30 something.
So he did, I can't remember, he did the math, like if you look at our physical costs of the bottle, the printing, the shipping, our average refund rate, average chart, all those metrics all together came out, like our average cost per each sell was that, so we made 150, our average cost was this, that means we've got $120 or so that we're able to spend to break even, okay?
And there's, if I can spend $120 to break even, that's how you take a company, you take an offer, and that's how we went from $30,000 a month to $300,000 a month to $500,000 a month, and that's why we're stuck at this level till we get more merchant to get to the next level and the next level, because it's literally, you come out there, and if you can spend that much to acquire a customer, you don't have to know SEO or Facebook or PBC or anything, there's a thousand people you can go to, and just say, this is what I can spend, and they will drive you as much traffic as you can handle as long as you can keep paying the checks, and that's how we were able to start scaling it really, really quickly.

Have you started to check to see where it's going yet?

So we did postcard test first, we tested four or five postcards, Luke Jaden actually wrote them all for us.

We mailed those, and one of the four postcards actually did really well, it, I can't remember what it did, but it did really well.
So then we took that versus a tear sheet, you guys know what a tear sheet is?
A tear sheet's like an ad, looks like it was ripped out of a magazine, and they fold it up in a, like in a square, they put it in like a square envelope, and they put a little sticky note on it, and they mailed out, so it looks like your friend ripped out of a magazine and sent it to you.
So we did a tear sheet, and the tear sheet just thrashed the postcards, like I'll never do a postcard again.
This is our tear sheet, so it looked like, this is one of our testimonials, this is Jack, and we just had like, his testimonial written in there, and the backside of the sheet had some more testimonials, and a big huge call to action for people to call in, and the tear sheet just beat it a lot.
So we stopped doing this because we weren't focusing on the continuity, and I hadn't figured out yet how to do the call center, how to sell the hire ticket, so we're still kind of playing with that before we run these again, but, I don't know, direct email's fun, I like that a lot, so.
Yeah.
You got a affiliate program?
Yep, yep, we have a affiliate program, and yeah, I mean, affiliates, there's media buyers, when you know your cost-pracery position, you can say, we can just pay $85, or $80 for every person you bring us, man, there are unlimited people that'll line up that will drive you traffic for that, yeah.
It all comes back to building this.
Now you guys, again, this is the same concept whether you are in info marketing, physical products, whatever you're doing, this is the same process we go through, okay, when we're launching a new offer, we're launching DotCom Secrets Labs, we're launching whatever, same thing, getting the offer out there, what's the upsell, what's the downsell, driving traffic, figuring out our numbers, figure out what our cart value is, and figure out what our cart value is, minus our hard cost, now we know what it is, I can come back to John and say, John, I know that for every person who buys DCS Labs book, now we're making $50, our hard costs are 10, so we got $40, so you can spend up to $28 or $30 to acquire customers on Facebook, and he can go in there and just drive ads all day long.
I can go to affiliates and say, hey, for every book you give away, I'm going to pay you $20 or $30 for every free book you give away, because I know every book I give away, I'm going to make $50.
Do you see how that works?
When you start learning this process, and all the stuff we've talked about from last event on building sales funnels, and this event with the traffic, it all comes down to just understanding these pieces, and when you have them in place, and you know your metrics, then this game becomes really, really easy, it's not difficult at that point.
You can find people to buy traffic, you can find affiliates, you can find people to do that, but you got to know what these numbers are first, and as soon as you got that, then this game becomes really fun and really easy.
All right, so that's kind of where we're at with NURSL.

Now, I want to tell you guys kind of the story real quick behind this supplement, because this is the next one that we have been working on, and I think there's some things in this that are going to be interesting to you, because how we're going to be marketing, it's way different than this.
And it's going to tie back into this whole expert thing I want to weave everything back with here at the end.
So this is a supplement, my trainer created this a couple of years ago, and it was really, really good.
He tried to sell it online, it had some success, lost his merchant accounts and just gave up on it.
So I started working out with him, and I was like, we started talking about business, and he was like, oh, I made a supplement one time, and we started talking about it, and eventually we decided, let's make the supplement again, and let's bring it back from the dead.
So we brought it back from the dead, and you guys have tasted it, it tastes like candy, it's awesome, it gives you exactly what you need.
And we're trying to get out the right way to brand it, and I totally screwed up on this.
And so one thing I would recommend is doing your homework before you pick your name.
Like I decided to call MMA Ignite, because I was like, I thought the MMA would encompass all of the martial arts in one, but I didn't realize until I talked to Lloyd Irving, who's one of the biggest MMA guys in the country, was it was like the exact opposite, like calling it MMA, alienated every other martial art, and I was like, crap.
And so we kind of had this thing now where we couldn't sell it, and I was like, we got a really good product, I don't like the brownie, but I want to figure out how to sell it, but I don't know the right way to do it.
So it's been sitting on our shelves for like six months, just trying to think like, what's the right thing to do, what's the best way?
And then a little while ago, I saw this page from Luke, one of my buddies, and it just, it gave me, it opened up the whole world for me on the supplement, so I'm gonna show you it's kind of the concept.
So this is, he has launched this green company, they're doing really, really well with it right now.
And again, when you're starting at any of these things, like look what other people are doing and model to begin with.
But I saw it on Facebook because he was offering these free samples.
Check it out.
So if you go to basicgreen.com slash free samples, you go in here and you just put in your address, you don't have to pay anything, no credit card, and he ships this thing out, ships you out a little sample pack of it, and boom, you've got it.
And then from there, he's got a whole sequence where he tries to sell you monthly, and subscriptions and all those kind of things.
I was like, that is just, that is like the coolest thing in the world.
So right now Ricky's going through the process where we're gonna ship him all of our leftover MMA Ignite, and he's gonna put them into like little packets like this.
And what we're gonna do is, instead of calling it MMA Ignite, we're just gonna change the name to Ignite, and then that way I can do some really cool branding stuff.
So this is a strategy to think about as you're kind of creating your supplements.
So we're gonna call it just Ignite, and what I'm gonna do is we're gonna be able to target specific groups on Facebook and everything else we're doing, but target very specifically.
So for example, I can go to all the wrestlers, and I can talk about this new product called Wrestlers Ignite.
This is the thing that's gonna ignite your wrestling.
Okay, I can go to all the gamers who are playing video games till three in the morning when their parents want me to go to bed, and they get really tired.
I can get them gamer Ignite.
I can target all the gamers.
And I can target all of the Jiu Jitsu guys.
I can target all the marketers.
I can target all these cool different people by doing that.
Is that exciting?
So that's kind of our strategy.
You guys will see this roll out probably in the next little bit.
You'll probably see marketers Ignite, but the core product we're selling is just Ignite.
But it'll say that on the image, and when we ship out it'll just be Ignite.
And that's how we're gonna do it like this, where it's giving away free samples, and it created a cool funnel where you give away a free sample, and the next page is like, hey, if you want a $20 discount, share this free sample on your fan page, and boom, they'll start sharing it, and they'll start sharing it, and we're just gonna get tons of free samples out there and start building our list of customers.
That kind of a fun way to market a supplement though?
Yeah.
Yeah, I'll just have my designers add gamers on the side, and then marketers and fishers and hunters, and who else could use some pre-workout energy buzz?
All right, babysitter Ignite.
All right.
All right, cool.
All right, so that's some of the stuff.
So this is kind of the brain of everything we've done.
Now I wanna step back now because if I was to start over right now from scratch, where some of you guys are doing it right now, I wanna tell you what I would do differently, because I wanna shortcut this path for you, okay?
I talked yesterday all about the whole expert stuff, right?
If I was to do a supplement again, my biggest frustration with what I did with my supplement with Nuricell is that I don't care about anything that these people care about.
We flew into our office, they're the nicest people in the whole world, but they're talking about stuff that my grandparents would love them, and they would hang out with them, but I don't relate these people.
I have no information I can share with people about neuropathy, about diabetes, about any of these issues, because I don't relate to that at all.
And I look at someone we talked yesterday about Dave Asbury, right, with the Bulletproof Executive, where he's built a huge following of people through his expert, through his information, through all this stuff, and then he comes in like, what product, what supplement can I give these people that's gonna help change their life?
Okay, and he comes out with Bulletproof Coffee, and he gives MCT oil, and all of a sudden it's all these things he can do, and then he comes out with Gabba Wave, and he comes out with thing after thing after thing, and this hoard of people are buying everything.
His sales pitch is not hard at all.
He doesn't have to have amazing copies, he doesn't have anything, because what he's doing is he's built the try, he's built the herd ahead of time, and he's explained to him the benefits of these things that he offers his brand.
I don't drink coffee, yet I buy two or three quarts of MCT oil from him every single month, because I'm sold on, I need MCT oil and everything I do.
On his checkout page, it's like, hey, buy one bottle, or do you want an auto-ship?
I'm like, yeah, I might as well auto-ships.
I don't forget, because I don't wanna miss this thing out.
And it's just kind of an interesting different thing.
When I look in the marketing world, if you're in my market, the internet marketing market, what are the things in my market people are talking about right now?
My market's talking about energy, about biohacking, about ways to make your brain smarter.
So for me, if I was gonna build another supplement business, what I would be focusing on is something that I'd have the whole new tropics angle, because that's something that my existing market, I could sell to him.
So for you guys, one thing about, some of you guys are gonna want your supplements as a front end business, and it's a great front end business.
But I would look more at what is it that you're already creating?
What is the, what's the, what product are you selling?
What information, what thing are you already building an audience with?
And then from there, what are the supplements?
What are the things that you can offer those people to help amplify whatever it is you're teaching?
That would be the direction I would definitely go, because now you can go and you can create tons of information products, tons of front ends, and back ends, and upsells, and take someone from this to coaching, and it becomes one of many of your products in your product line.
And that gets me more excited personally if I was to start over from scratch.
That's our biggest problem we have this right now, is like nobody in our office knows anything about diabetes.
How do we write follow up sequences for people?
I don't know.
We lucked out, we got one of my friends, Mike Geary, started writing our odd responder sequences.
Like we honestly didn't mail our customers anything for like a year.
We've got 30,000 customers right now, we hadn't mailed them anything.
So he logged in, he just mails them stuff all the time, and we get commissions off it.
But they were just sitting there, right?
Because it's not a market I'm passionate about.
So for you guys, I think about like, if you're doing this, do it in a market you're passionate about, because if you do that, it can become a really cool addition to everything else you're doing.
So that's kind of my first big thing.
The second thing is I would make sure when you're creating this kind of thing, there is upfront costs when you're doing it, especially if you're manufacturing your own, there's upfront costs involved.
Right now we were working with Drew at FitLife TV and they're launching their new green drink, which is really good by the way, it tastes like candy.
It's really, really good.
And the strategy I kind of told them, I said, look, you guys are gonna go order and spend 10, 15,000 dollars getting all this stuff.
But what if you already have an audience of like millions and millions of people, what if you just have you showing it and say, hey, in six months for an hour, in a month for an hour, six weeks now, we're about to do this, but we wanna know real quick ahead of time, like who actually wants this?
So if you're interested, then come pre-order a whole bunch of stuff for us, and that way we know how much to get.
And that way they get all the money ahead of time before they ever place the order, which just speeds up that entire process.
So think about that, if you've already got an audience, like I could come out tomorrow basically, and I could email my entire list and say, hey, we have this new, new tropics product coming out, this is the benefits, why it's awesome, takes me six weeks to build it, and then after we build this, it can be like six weeks more later to get it.
So if you want it right now, come and pay the 50 bucks or the 100 bucks for the bottle now, and that way I get all this cash coming in ahead of time, then I can go make the order and then ship it out to them.
That way you are lowering your risk on that as well.
And you can only really do that though, if you've already got an existing audience that knows you, they like you, they trust you.
And then one other cool strategy I wanna share, because I don't know how this fits into anything, but it's really cool, I was at a Joe Polish event a little while ago, and he used to do a bunch of marketing for Bill Phillips, like he was one of the main marketing guys on his team, he was sharing some of their war stories, and one of the coolest stories he told, he said that one time they had ordered a whole bunch of protein, and I think they had screwed up and they ordered way too much of it.
So they had these huge trucks out there in their warehouse with all these supplements, and they were just like, what are we gonna do with all this stuff?
And so they went and they basically took it and they wrote this huge sales letter, and they had a picture of these two trucks of protein, they're like, hey, we accidentally ordered way too much protein, if you want some we're gonna give you a discount, whatever, and they took that letter, and they melded it out to the entire muscle media subscription base, and they sold just millions of dollars with the protein.
And then a month later, Joe had the idea of like, you know that promotion was awesome, he said, what if we had the exact same letter, but this time we just crossed out one of the things and said one down, one to go, and then sent out the exact same sales letter.
So they did that instead of the exact same sales letter, I think he said that they did like two or three times as much money on the second sales letter as they did the first one.
So there's some really cool things you can do, like blow out your inventory, and to do cool things like that that are pretty fun.
The other thing I would look at, just another random strategy, is we started putting our supplement on Amazon, and we've had really good success with that.
The nice thing about Amazon is typically you're not selling with like a sales letter on Amazon, it's just kind of there, and you can rank it for the keywords and things like that.
And the nice thing about that is, the issues you have when you have a long form sales letter is you have all these words in there, and lawyers look at those words, and they have issues all the time with different things.
When you're in Amazon, usually you have an image and like a paragraph, and it's a lot harder to get in trouble for claims and stuff like that, and like three sentences, and you can in like a 30 page sales letter.
And so I would almost start a lot of times now, if I was to start over, is just putting this on Amazon, testing that way first, having the paragraph, driving some sales, and I've got friends right now that are doing over $10,000 a month, just selling a supplement on Amazon.
No sales letter, no marketing, no anything, just doing that.
And that's a strategy we'll cover in depth in the supplement course that we're gonna do with you guys.
Yeah, by the way, the first name we had for NURSL was neuropathy treatment.
That was the name of the product, do you remember that?
So we totally like, yeah, had a claim in our name, which was bad.
I was just asking to get arrested at that point, so that was bad.
Yeah, good feedback.
Okay, so to kind of wrap this up, what I would say, this is the process for you guys, if you wanna kind of get involved in the supplement game, what I would go through.
The first thing, again, as I recommend, is make sure you're picking an industry, you're picking a supplement, something that you're passionate about, so you're not stuck kind of in this dead-end thing like I am where I have nothing to say to these people.
Find something you're actually passionate about because then it can become part of your entire business structure, which is exciting.
Every email you're sending out can be pushing people back, every training you're doing can be pushing people.
My favorite thing about Dotcom Cegers and ClickFunnels is everything that Dotcom Cegers does, moving forward after you launch ClickFunnels, will somehow be pushing back to ClickFunnels.
They feed each other.
It's a fuel, this fuel, it doesn't cost me any money at all, it keeps driving this.
So that's a good question.
Kind of, but at the same time, for me and I think for all of you guys, there's a path, I wouldn't have been ready for 10 years ago, you know what I mean?
I don't know.

And then we've got two people now full-time who do that.
I'd say probably 23% of the support comes through phone and the other ones come through email.
We tried really hard to just only do email, we didn't want to do phones.
But again, it'd be shocked how higher percentage of our people don't even have an email address.
Like the fields come in and their email address says, I don't have an email.
You don't have an email?
Like, do you know what year this is?
But that's still by.
So we have to have phone support, even though we've tried multiple times to not have it.
So it just depends on your market.
I think like in, like I look at like Dave with the bullet proof executive, like he wouldn't have, I don't think you'd have to have an inbound phone support because most of his, you know, his target market are all like highly productive entrepreneurs, executives, things like that.
